action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 7's scaled-up MLP (hidden=1024, dropout=0.3, label_clip=0.1) achieved ic_mean=0.0111, worse than the 3-epoch small MLP baseline (0.0153). The training curve shows val_loss ~0.9965 (essentially flat), suggesting the model is predicting near zero and learning almost nothing. Analysis: A key suspicion is that label clipping at ±0.1 may be catastrophic if Qlib's handler has z-scored labels (std≈1), or the model is severely underfitting due to other issues. We need to inspect the raw label distribution before making changes. Decision: Run a quick diagnostic script to compute label mean, std, min, max on the training set. Then fix train.py based on findings.
